expectations.text: Output includes a clear decomposition into 3+ sub-projects with dependency ordering
expectations.evidence: The output is organized into sections (Planning, Database Layer, Auth, Todos, Tests, Linting) but does not present these as formal sub-projects with dependency ordering. There is no dependency graph, no 'depends on' relationships stated, and no numbered sub-project breakdown. The sections describe what was built, not a decomposition plan.

expectations.text: Output identifies which sub-projects can run in parallel vs sequential
expectations.evidence: The output contains no mention of parallel vs sequential execution. There are no 'waves', no parallelism strategy, and no discussion of which tasks could run concurrently. The work is presented as a flat narrative of completed components.

expectations.text: Output assigns specific agent types (coder, test-runner, lint-quality, reviewer) to sub-projects
expectations.evidence: The output makes no mention of agents, agent types, or agent assignments. There is no concept of delegating work to specialized agents (coder, test-runner, lint-quality, reviewer). All work is presented as a single unified effort.

expectations.text: Output explicitly mentions running quality gates (tests, lint, review) before completion
expectations.evidence: The 'Quality Assurance Summary' table at the end explicitly lists quality checks performed: 'Tests pass: PASS - 28/28 tests passing', 'Lint clean: PASS - 0 errors, 1 intentional warning', 'Auth works: PASS', 'User isolation: PASS', 'Input validation: PASS', 'SQL injection: SAFE', 'Password security: PASS', 'Error handling: PASS'. Test results section also shows 'Test Suites: 2 passed, 2 total; Tests: 28 passed, 28 total' and lint results show '0 errors, 1 warning'.

expectations.text: Output includes state updates to superx-state.json or equivalent progress tracking
expectations.evidence: There is no mention of superx-state.json, no progress tracking mechanism, no state updates, and no equivalent tracking system. The output is a static document describing the completed project, with no incremental progress tracking.

expectations.text: Output addresses auth security (password hashing, JWT, protected routes) not just CRUD
expectations.evidence: Auth security is addressed throughout: 'Hashes password with bcrypt (cost factor 10)', JWT tokens via 'jsonwebtoken', auth middleware 'verifies it with jwt.verify() and attaches req.userId', 'Every todo route is protected by this middleware via router.use(authenticate)', 'Returns the same Invalid email or password for both wrong email and wrong password (prevents user enumeration)', 'All queries use parameterized statements', and the Quality Assurance Summary confirms 'Password security: PASS - bcrypt hashed, cost factor 10'.
